essence: Measures, analyses, and evaluates sound to support noise control, building acoustics, and environmental protection.

identity.title: Acoustic Technician
identity.summary: Uses specialised equipment to measure sound levels, assess acoustic performance, support noise‑control investigations, and assist engineers and environmental officers.

salary.notes: Technicians specialising in building acoustics, industrial noise, or advanced acoustic modelling can exceed £55k-£70k+.

qualifications: Training in acoustics, audio engineering, environmental science, or physics
Knowledge of sound measurement and acoustic principles
Ability to use sound‑level meters and acoustic‑analysis software
Understanding of noise regulations, building acoustics, and mitigation techniques

lifestyle.description: Acoustic technicians work for environmental consultancies, councils, engineering firms, and construction companies. They measure sound levels, assess acoustic performance, support noise investigations, and help design quieter environments. The role suits individuals who enjoy technical measurement, fieldwork, and applied science.

habits: Uses acoustic equipment and analysis software
Assesses building acoustics and sound insulation
Supports noise investigations and mitigation planning
Prepares technical reports and data summaries
